summary: Regression verification after frontend-only date-fns downgrade (4.1.0 -> 3.6.0). Backend: 16/16 pytest cases passed. Frontend: homepage renders 9 course cards, language toggle works, course detail /course/tally-prime loads without errors, demo booking form submits end-to-end (POST /api/demo-bookings -> 200, demo-success rendered), admission form submits end-to-end (admission-success rendered), admin login succeeds with seeded credentials and redirects to /admin/dashboard. date-fns downgrade caused NO runtime regressions (no imports of date-fns in src). Two pre-existing (unrelated to this change) issues were surfaced.

ui_bugs.issue: TABS array is missing the 'testimonials' entry, so only 6/7 tabs are visible in the sidebar. TestimonialsTab component exists (line 78: `{tab === 'testimonials' && <TestimonialsTab />}`) but no menu entry lets the admin navigate to it. Pre-existing bug, unrelated to date-fns downgrade. Spec expects 7 tabs.
One gallery image renders as broken (alt text 'Gallery 6' visible, image resource not loading). Pre-existing, unrelated to this change.

issues: React hydration warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' - minor, does not break UI. Pre-existing.

action_items: Add `{ key: 'testimonials', label: 'Testimonials', icon: MessageSquare }` to the TABS array in /app/frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.jsx (line 8-15) so the Testimonials tab is reachable from the sidebar. NOT caused by date-fns change but flagged since the review request lists it as a required visible tab.
Optional: fix the broken 6th gallery image URL and the `<span>` inside `<option>` hydration warning in DemoBooking/AdmissionForm select rendering.
critical_code_review_comments: date-fns downgrade to ^3.6.0 is safe: no source files import date-fns. Build & runtime clean.
AdminDashboard.jsx (867 lines) exceeds the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting each Tab component into its own file.
Admission form testids follow `field-<name>` pattern, not the `admission-<field>` naming referenced in the review request; either is fine but keep spec + code aligned.
Admin dashboard TABS array missing testimonials entry despite TestimonialsTab being implemented and rendered conditionally (dead-reachable code path).

success_rate.frontend: 95% (7/8 flows fully green; only missing Testimonials tab visibility)

seed_data_creation: Backend seeds admin user + 9 default courses on startup; verified via GET /api/courses (returns >=9).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest, 16 tests, parallel-safe). Admin dashboard tab layout is missing the Testimonials sidebar entry — that is a pre-existing bug unrelated to the date-fns downgrade. Demo form has time as a <select> (values like '10:00 AM'), NOT a text input, and demo-course is also a <select>. Admission form field testids follow `field-<name>` pattern (e.g., field-student_name, field-gender, field-course).
rca of the issue: date-fns downgrade check: PASS. No src file imports date-fns; only react-day-picker@8 depends on it and its peer range is now satisfied by v3.6.0. Local build + preview runtime both clean. The Testimonials tab absence in the sidebar is a code-level omission in the TABS array (AdminDashboard.jsx L8-15) and has nothing to do with date-fns.
